# mail

```go
import "net/mail"
```

Package mail implements parsing of mail messages.

For the most part, this package follows the syntax as specified by RFC 5322 and extended by RFC 6532. Notable divergences:

- Obsolete address formats are not parsed, including addresses with embedded route information.
- The full range of spacing \(the CFWS syntax element\) is not supported, such as breaking addresses across lines.
- No unicode normalization is performed.
- A leading From line is permitted, as in mbox format \(RFC 4155\).

<a name="ParseDate"></a>

## func [ParseDate](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L148>)

```go
func ParseDate(date string) (time.Time, error)
```

ParseDate parses an RFC 5322 date string.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"net/mail"
	"time"
)

func main() {
	dateStr := "Wed, 09 Oct 2024 09:55:06 -0700"

	t, err := mail.ParseDate(dateStr)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("Failed to parse date: %v", err)
	}

	fmt.Println(t.Format(time.RFC3339))

}
```

#### Output

```go
2024-10-09T09:55:06-07:00
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Address"></a>

## type [Address](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L231-L234>)

Address represents a single mail address. An address such as "Barry Gibbs \<bg@example.com\>" is represented as Address\{Name: "Barry Gibbs", Address: "bg@example.com"\}.

```go
type Address struct {
    Name    string // Proper name; may be empty.
    Address string // user@domain
}
```

<a name="ParseAddress"></a>

### func [ParseAddress](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L237>)

```go
func ParseAddress(address string) (*Address, error)
```

ParseAddress parses a single RFC 5322 address, e.g. "Barry Gibbs \<bg@example.com\>"

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"net/mail"
)

func main() {
	e, err := mail.ParseAddress("Alice <alice@example.com>")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	fmt.Println(e.Name, e.Address)

}
```

#### Output

```go
Alice alice@example.com
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ParseAddressList"></a>

### func [ParseAddressList](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L242>)

```go
func ParseAddressList(list string) ([]*Address, error)
```

ParseAddressList parses the given string as a list of addresses.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"net/mail"
)

func main() {
	const list = "Alice <alice@example.com>, Bob <bob@example.com>, Eve <eve@example.com>"
	emails, err := mail.ParseAddressList(list)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	for _, v := range emails {
		fmt.Println(v.Name, v.Address)
	}

}
```

#### Output

```go
Alice alice@example.com
Bob bob@example.com
Eve eve@example.com
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Address.String"></a>

### func \(\*Address\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L267>)

```go
func (a *Address) String() string
```

String formats the address as a valid RFC 5322 address. If the address's name contains non\-ASCII characters the name will be rendered according to RFC 2047.

## type [AddressParser](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L247-L250>)

An AddressParser is an RFC 5322 address parser.

```go
type AddressParser struct {
    // WordDecoder optionally specifies a decoder for RFC 2047 encoded-words.
    WordDecoder *mime.WordDecoder
}
```

<a name="AddressParser.Parse"></a>

### func \(\*AddressParser\) [Parse](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L254>)

```go
func (p *AddressParser) Parse(address string) (*Address, error)
```

Parse parses a single RFC 5322 address of the form "Gogh Fir \<gf@example.com\>" or "foo@example.com".

<a name="AddressParser.ParseList"></a>

### func \(\*AddressParser\) [ParseList](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L260>)

```go
func (p *AddressParser) ParseList(list string) ([]*Address, error)
```

ParseList parses the given string as a list of comma\-separated addresses of the form "Gogh Fir \<gf@example.com\>" or "foo@example.com".

<a name="Header"></a>

## type [Header](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L196>)

A Header represents the key\-value pairs in a mail message header.

```go
type Header map[string][]string
```

<a name="Header.AddressList"></a>

### func \(Header\) [AddressList](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L220>)

```go
func (h Header) AddressList(key string) ([]*Address, error)
```

AddressList parses the named header field as a list of addresses.

<a name="Header.Date"></a>

### func \(Header\) [Date](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L211>)

```go
func (h Header) Date() (time.Time, error)
```

Date parses the Date header field.

<a name="Header.Get"></a>

### func \(Header\) [Get](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L204>)

```go
func (h Header) Get(key string) string
```

Get gets the first value associated with the given key. It is case insensitive; CanonicalMIMEHeaderKey is used to canonicalize the provided key. If there are no values associated with the key, Get returns "". To access multiple values of a key, or to use non\-canonical keys, access the map directly.

<a name="Message"></a>

## type [Message](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L46-L49>)

A Message represents a parsed mail message.

```go
type Message struct {
    Header Header
    Body   io.Reader
}
```

<a name="ReadMessage"></a>

### func [ReadMessage](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/mail/message.go#L54>)

```go
func ReadMessage(r io.Reader) (msg *Message, err error)
```

ReadMessage reads a message from r. The headers are parsed, and the body of the message will be available for reading from msg.Body.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"log"
	"net/mail"
	"strings"
)

func main() {
	msg := `Date: Mon, 23 Jun 2015 11:40:36 -0400
From: Gopher <from@example.com>
To: Another Gopher <to@example.com>
Subject: Gophers at Gophercon

Message body
`

	r := strings.NewReader(msg)
	m, err := mail.ReadMessage(r)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	header := m.Header
	fmt.Println("Date:", header.Get("Date"))
	fmt.Println("From:", header.Get("From"))
	fmt.Println("To:", header.Get("To"))
	fmt.Println("Subject:", header.Get("Subject"))

	body, err := io.ReadAll(m.Body)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	fmt.Printf("%s", body)

}
```

#### Output

```go
Date: Mon, 23 Jun 2015 11:40:36 -0400
From: Gopher <from@example.com>
To: Another Gopher <to@example.com>
Subject: Gophers at Gophercon
Message body
```

</p>
</details>
